metadata.title: Find the Pruning Cliff with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: Hello. This is Liam, in for Bear. Remove 20 percent of a network's channels and lose under 1 percent accuracy. Remove 70 percent and it falls off a cliff. The whole game is knowing where the cliff is.

props.command: claude "write pruning_curve.py: accuracy vs % channels structurally pruned, 0-90%. Shape: ~flat to 20%, 2-4% loss by 40%, 5-10% by 60%, collapse past 70%. Mark the 20-40% sweet spot and the >70% cliff."
props.segment: Find the Pruning Cliff with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: The book's accuracy-versus-pruning-rate curve. The cliff past seventy percent is real — our CLI script finds where the curve goes steep before you commit to a pruning target.

beats.act: MANIM — Flat region: accuracy holds to 20%
beats.narration_text: Start the sweep at 0 percent pruning. Accuracy holds flat — the network has redundancy to spare. Through 20 percent, accuracy loss is under 1 percent. The network doesn't even notice the channels are gone.

beats.act: MANIM — Curve steepens: sweet spot at 20-40%
beats.narration_text: Between 20 and 40 percent, accuracy starts to fall — 2 to 4 percent loss. Still acceptable for most applications. This is the sweet spot: meaningful compression, manageable accuracy trade. Terracotta shading marks the zone.

Past 70 percent, the curve collapses. Loss becomes 10, 20, 30 percent — unusable. The network has been pruned past its load-bearing structure. The cliff is sharp: one more percent of pruning can mean catastrophic failure.

Your turn. Ask Claude: write pruning_curve.py — accuracy versus percent channels structurally pruned, from 0 to 90 percent. Shape: roughly flat to 20 percent, 2 to 4 percent loss by 40 percent, 5 to 10 percent loss by 60 percent, collapse past 70 percent. Mark the 20 to 40 percent sweet spot and the greater-than-70-percent cliff. Once you have the curve, challenge yourself to overlay unstructured pruning on the same plot — and annotate that unstructured gives the same accuracy curve but never reduces latency on embedded hardware.

props.command: claude "write pruning_curve.py: accuracy vs % channels structurally pruned, 0-90%. Shape: ~flat to 20%, 2-4% loss by 40%, 5-10% by 60%, collapse past 70%. Mark the 20-40% sweet spot and the >70% cliff."

props.title: Find the Pruning Cliff with Claude Code
